author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice
book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

metadata.author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice

title: Canon XVI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: That it does not become one in holy orders to be clad in costly apparel.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: All buffoonery and decking of the body ill becomes the priestly rank.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Therefore those bishops and clerics who array themselves in gay and showy clothing ought to correct themselves, and if they do not amend they ought to be subjected to punishment.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: So likewise they who anoint themselves with perfumes.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: When the root of bitterness sprang up, there was poured into the Catholic Church the pollution of the heresy of the traducers of the Christians.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: And such as were defiled by it, not only detested the pictured images, but also set at naught all decorum, being exceedingly mad against those who lived gravely and religiously; so that in them was fulfilled that which is written, “The service of God is abominable to the sinner.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: If therefore, any are found deriding those who are clad in poor and grave raiment, let them be corrected by punishment.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: For from early times every man in holy orders wore modest and grave clothing; and verily whatever is worn, not so much because of necessity, as for the sake of outward show, savours of dandyism, as says Basil the Great.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Nor did anyone array himself in raiment embroidered with silk, nor put many coloured ornaments on the border of his garments; for they had heard from the lips of God that “They that wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon XVI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Bishops and clergymen arraying themselves in splendid clothes and anointed with perfumes must be corrected.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Should they persist, they must be punished.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Balsamon and Zonaras tell of the magnificence in dress assumed by some of the superior clergy among the Iconoclasts, wearing stuffs woven with threads of gold, and their loins girt with golden girdles, and sentences embroidered in gold on the edge of their raiment.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It is curious to note how often heretics fall into extremes.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: We have seen how Eustathius wore a conspicuous garb and was not willing to appear in the ordinary dress of a clergyman of his day.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: His was the one extreme of ultra clerical or, I should say, ascetic clothing.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: These Iconoclasts went to the other extreme and dressed themselves like men of the world, giving themselves the dandy airs of the fops of the day, thus, as always, making themselves ridiculous in the eyes of the wise, and their office contemptible in the eyes of the common people.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: II., Causa XXI., Quæst.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: IV., canon j.
